content.cn_text: [旁白]: 现在翻到第5页的第3部分。
content.en_text: [Narrator]: Now turn to section 3 on page 5.

content.cn_text: 第3部分
content.en_text: Section 3

content.cn_text: 你将听到一个名叫海伦的学生和她的导师讨论一份作业。
content.en_text: You will hear a discussion between a student called Helen and her tutor about an assignment

content.cn_text: 海伦正在完成的作业。
content.en_text: that Helen is working on.

content.cn_text: 首先，你有一些时间查看第5页的第21至24题。
content.en_text: First you have some time to look at questions 21 to 24 on page 5.

content.cn_text: 现在请仔细听并回答第21至24题。
content.en_text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 21 to 24.

content.cn_text: [导师]: 请进，海伦，我能帮你什么？
content.en_text: [Tutor]: Come in Helen, how can I help you?

content.cn_text: [海伦]: 嗯，我正在为人类学作业做研究，希望能核对一些细节。
content.en_text: [Helen]: Well, I'm doing research for the anthropology assignment and I was hoping to check a few

content.cn_text: 细节。
content.en_text: details.

content.cn_text: [导师]: 当然。
content.en_text: [Tutor]: Sure.

content.cn_text: 你选的是一件很好的太平洋岛屿的塔帕布，对吗？
content.en_text: You chose the top of a good Pacific Island top of cloth, didn't you?

content.cn_text: 你目前发现了什么？
content.en_text: What if you found out so far?

content.cn_text: [海伦]: 嗯，我打算这样介绍我的作业：塔帕布是一种面料，
content.en_text: [Helen]: Well, I was going to introduce my assignment by saying that the top of cloth is a fabric

content.cn_text: 由树皮制成，只是树的外层。
content.en_text: made from back, just the outer layer of the trees.

content.cn_text: 它在太平洋岛屿尤为常见，但并非仅限于此。
content.en_text: It's particularly common in the Pacific Islands, but not exclusive to them.

content.cn_text: 事实上，世界上许多其他民族也用树皮制作了高质量的布料，但
content.en_text: In fact, many other peoples around the world have made high quality cloth from back, but

content.cn_text: 太平洋塔帕布的独特之处在于它在该地区扮演的各种各样的角色。
content.en_text: what sets Pacific Tapper apart is the incredible variety of roles it's played in this region.

content.cn_text: [导师]: 嗯，不错的介绍，不过我觉得你可以在日期方面更具体一些。
content.en_text: [Tutor]: Yes, nice introduction, though I think you could be more specific regarding dates.

content.cn_text: 好的，那么使用的原材料呢？
content.en_text: OK, so what about the raw materials used?

content.cn_text: [海伦]: 嗯，塔帕布是由几种树木制成的，在太平洋地区，构树
content.en_text: [Helen]: Well, Tapper cloth is made from several species of tree, and the Pacific, the paper mulberry

content.cn_text: 最为常见，但它并非在所有条件下都能茁壮成长。
content.en_text: tree is most common, but it doesn't thrive in all conditions.

content.cn_text: 事实上，它最初并非在岛屿上发现，而是由第一批移民乘独木舟带入的。
content.en_text: In fact, it wasn't originally found in the islands, but was carried in their canoes

content.cn_text: 由第一批移民带入的。
content.en_text: by the first migrants.

content.cn_text: 塔帕布也由面包果树制成，这很方便，因为它的果实
content.en_text: The Tapper is also made from the bread fruit tree, which is convenient because it's fruit

content.cn_text: 是一种主食。
content.en_text: as a staple food.

content.cn_text: 然而，构树只用于制作塔帕布。
content.en_text: The paper mulberry tree is only grown for Tapper making, though.

content.cn_text: [导师]: 嗯，很好。
content.en_text: [Tutor]: Yes, that's good.

content.cn_text: 那么新西兰的毛利人呢？
content.en_text: Now what about the Maori people here in New Zealand?

content.cn_text: [海伦]: 但是毛利人现在不制作塔帕布了。
content.en_text: [Helen]: But the Maori don't make Tapper now.

content.cn_text: [导师]: 对，你需要解释一下原因。
content.en_text: [Tutor]: That's right, and you need to account for it.

content.cn_text: 我们知道，当毛利人从其他太平洋岛屿迁徙到这里时，他们准备
content.en_text: We know that when Maori migrated here from the other Pacific Islands, they were prepared

content.cn_text: 制作塔帕布，因为他们带来了构树。
content.en_text: to make Tapper because they brought the paper mulberry tree with them.

content.cn_text: 问题是，他们在新西兰居住一段时间后，发现了亚麻植物，它
content.en_text: The thing was, after they'd been in New Zealand a bit, they found the flax plant, which

content.cn_text: 比塔帕布更优越，因为它能制作出更结实的布料。
content.en_text: is superior to Tapper because it makes a stronger fabric.

content.cn_text: 到18世纪欧洲人到来时，毛利人已经用亚麻制作所有的布料了
content.en_text: By the time Europeans arrived in the 18th century, Maori were making all their fabric from

content.cn_text: 并且已经持续了一段时间。
content.en_text: flax and had been for some time.

content.cn_text: [海伦]: 好的，那么制作过程本身呢，首先用木锤敲打树皮内层
content.en_text: [Helen]: OK, so with the production process itself, first at the inner bark is beaten with wooden

content.cn_text: 使其纤维软化。
content.en_text: hammers to soften the fibres.

content.cn_text: 然后将不同的碎片用葛粉制成的粘合剂粘合在一起。
content.en_text: Then the different pieces are glued together using an adhesive paste made from the arrowroot

content.cn_text: 葛粉。
content.en_text: tuba.

content.cn_text: 这是制作大块布料的唯一方法，因为树皮纤维太细，
content.en_text: This is the only way to fabricate large pieces of cloth because bark strands are too fine

content.cn_text: 无法编织在一起，而且缝合也不够牢固。
content.en_text: to be woven together and stitching isn't strong enough.

content.cn_text: [旁白]: 在你听完剩余的讨论之前，你有一些时间查看第25
content.en_text: [Narrator]: Before you hear the rest of the discussion, you have some time to look at questions 25

content.cn_text: 至30题，在第6页。
content.en_text: to 30 on page 6.

content.cn_text: 现在，请听并回答第25至30题。
content.en_text: Now, listen and answer questions 25 to 30.

content.cn_text: [导师]: 那么现在你需要不同国家的详细信息。
content.en_text: [Tutor]: So now you need details about different countries.

content.cn_text: [海伦]: 你会从哪里开始？
content.en_text: [Helen]: Where would you start?

content.cn_text: [导师]: 我想萨摩亚是显而易见的起点。
content.en_text: [Tutor]:I think Samoa is the obvious place.

content.cn_text: 它以其名为“西阿坡”的精美布料闻名，上面手绘着祖先的形象。
content.en_text: It's famous for its very fine cloth called Seattle, which is hand-painted with representations

content.cn_text: 祖先的形象。
content.en_text: of the ancestors.

content.cn_text: 直到今天，在生活中最重要的事件，如出生、葬礼、婚礼
content.en_text: Still today, at the most profound events in life such as births, funerals, weddings

content.cn_text: 以及酋长的授职仪式上，萨摩亚人都会穿着西阿坡长袍，以增加仪式的意义和重要性。
content.en_text: and the investiture of chiefs, Samoans were siapo robes to add significance and meaning

content.cn_text: 和意义。
content.en_text: to the ceremony.

content.cn_text: [海伦]: 好的，然后我可以说说汤加。
content.en_text: [Helen]: OK, then I could talk about Tonga.

content.cn_text: 在我看来，汤加的一大创新是开发出一种简单、粗糙的
content.en_text: It seems to me that the great innovation in Tonga has been developing a simple, coarse

content.cn_text: 布料，制作快捷方便。
content.en_text: cloth, which is quick and easy to make.

content.cn_text: 这适用于各种家庭日常用途，比如床罩、
content.en_text: This is suitable for all sorts of everyday functions around the house like bedcudders,

content.cn_text: 蚊帐和窗帘。
content.en_text: mosquito nets and curtains.

content.cn_text: [导师]: 说得好。
content.en_text: [Tutor]: Good point.

content.cn_text: 那么库克群岛的塔帕布呢？
content.en_text: Now, what about Cook Island's Tupper?

content.cn_text: [海伦]: 嗯，那里的土壤质量很差，所以经常使用面包果树。
content.en_text: [Helen]: Well, the soil there is poor quality, so the breadfruit tree is often used.

content.cn_text: 一种名为Tikoru的厚布，被缠绕在杆子上，用于标记
content.en_text: One type of thick cloth, called Tikoru, was wrapped around the poles and used to mark the

content.cn_text: 礼拜场所的入口，因此在当地文化中备受推崇。
content.en_text: entrances to places of worship so it was highly regarded in local culture.

content.cn_text: [导师]: 你也可以提一下斐济，这很有趣，因为塔帕布在那里实际上被用作货币。
content.en_text: [Tutor]: You might mention Fiji as well, which is interesting because Tupper was actually used as a currency

content.cn_text: 货币。
content.en_text: there.

content.cn_text: 斐济人过去常在岛屿之间航行，用塔帕布交换其他商品，比如
content.en_text: Fijians used to sail between the islands and exchange Tupper for other commodities like

content.cn_text: 独木舟或猪。
content.en_text: canoes or pigs.

content.cn_text: [海伦]: 我知道在塔希提，塔帕布是不同的，因为图案是彩色的，
content.en_text: [Helen]: I know that in Tahiti, the Tupper cloth is different because the patterns are in colour,

content.cn_text: 这被认为比通常的棕色图案更有价值。
content.en_text: which is considered more valuable than the usual brown patterns.

content.cn_text: [导师]: 人们写塔希提人使用彩色颜料，但它们并没有更高的价值。
content.en_text: [Tutor]: You are right about the Tahitians using coloured pigments, but they aren't more valuable.

content.cn_text: 这些颜色只是一种装饰。
content.en_text: The colours are only a decoration.

content.cn_text: 人们喜欢穿鲜艳的长袍，尤其是在跳舞和竞技游戏时，这样做
content.en_text: People enjoy wearing bright robes, especially for dancing and competitive games and do it

content.cn_text: 只是为了好玩。
content.en_text: just for fun.

content.cn_text: [海伦]: 哦，我会记下来。
content.en_text: [Helen]: Oh, I'll make a note of it.

content.cn_text: 嗯，我最后要提到的地方是蒂科皮亚。
content.en_text: Well, the last place I was going to mention was Tikorpia.

content.cn_text: 即使在今天，看到人们穿着塔帕布制成的衣服也很常见。
content.en_text: Even today it's common place to see people wearing clothes made of Tupper cloth.

content.cn_text: 在许多其他岛屿上，塔帕布只在特殊场合出现，但在这里
content.en_text: In many of the other islands, the Tupper only comes out on special occasions, but here

content.cn_text: 你会看到人们在花园里工作时穿着塔帕布。
content.en_text: you see people working in the gardens wearing Tupper.

content.cn_text: [导师]: 听起来很有希望，海伦。
content.en_text: [Tutor]: Sounds promising, Helen.

content.cn_text: 我期待阅读你的作业。
content.en_text: I'll look forward to reading your assignment.

content.cn_text: [旁白]: 第3部分到此结束。
content.en_text: [Narrator]: That is the end of section 3.

content.cn_text: 现在你有半分钟的时间检查你的答案。
content.en_text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.

groups.desc: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.
groups.title: Questions 21-24.

items.title: Pacific tapa cloth is different from other types of tapa cloth because it is

options.title: C
options.content: put to a wider range of uses

items.analysis:
答案解析：第21题的选项A“the only tapa made today”是一个干扰项。原文提到“not exclusive to them”（并非仅限于此），并且接下来会提到“many other peoples around the world have made high quality cloth from back”，这表明太平洋塔帕布并非是唯一现存或曾经存在的塔帕布，因此A是错误的。
答案解析：第21题的选项B“better quality than others”是一个干扰项。原文明确指出“many other peoples around the world have made high quality cloth from back”，这表明其他地区的塔帕布质量也很好，并未暗示太平洋塔帕布的质量比其他地区更好，因此B是错误的。
答案解析：第21题的正确答案是C“put to a wider range of uses”。原文明确指出“what sets Pacific Tapper apart is the incredible variety of roles it's played in this region”，这直接对应了选项C中“wider range of uses”（更广泛的用途），强调了太平洋塔帕布用途的广泛性是其独特之处。

items.title: What does Helen say about the paper mulberry tree?

options.title: A
options.content: It is also a source of food

options.title: B
options.content: It is not native to the Pacific

options.title: C
options.content: It can grow in any environment

items.analysis:
答案解析：第22题的选项C“It can grow in any environment”是一个干扰项。原文明确指出“but it doesn't thrive in all conditions”（但它并非在所有条件下都能茁壮成长），这与选项C的意思完全相反，因此C是错误的。
答案解析：第22题的正确答案是B“It is not native to the Pacific”。原文提到“it wasn't originally found in the islands, but was carried in their canoes by the first migrants”，这直接说明构树并非太平洋地区的原生植物，而是由早期移民带来的，符合选项B的描述。
答案解析：第22题的选项A“It is also a source of food”是一个干扰项。原文明确指出“The paper mulberry tree is only grown for Tapper making, though.”，并提到面包果树的果实是主食，通过对比说明构树并非食物来源，因此A是错误的。

items.title: Why did the Maori people of New Zealand stop making tapa?

options.title: A
options.content: They could not find the right trees in New Zealand

options.title: B
options.content: They were introduced to other fabrics by the Europeans

options.title: C
options.content: They found a better material for making fabric

items.analysis:
答案解析：第23题的选项A“They could not find the right trees in New Zealand”是一个干扰项。原文提到毛利人“brought the paper mulberry tree with them”，这说明他们最初是带来了制作塔帕布的原材料（构树），因此不能说他们找不到合适的树木，A是错误的。
答案解析：第23题的正确答案是C“They found a better material for making fabric”。原文指出毛利人发现了“flax plant, which is superior to Tapper because it makes a stronger fabric”，这直接表明他们找到了比塔帕布更好的材料（亚麻）来制作布料，符合选项C的描述。
答案解析：第23题的选项B“They were introduced to other fabrics by the Europeans”是一个干扰项。原文说“By the time Europeans arrived in the 18th century, Maori were making all their fabric from flax and had been for some time”，这意味着毛利人在欧洲人到来之前就已经开始使用亚麻制作布料了，而非由欧洲人引入，因此B是错误的。

items.title: Large pieces of tapa are made from smaller pieces which are
items.analysis:
答案解析：第24题的正确答案是A“stuck together”。原文明确指出“Then the different pieces are glued together using an adhesive paste”，其中“glued together”（粘合在一起）与选项A的“stuck together”（粘在一起）意思相同。
答案解析：第24题的选项B“woven together”（编织在一起）和C“sewn together”（缝合在一起）是干扰项。原文明确指出“bark strands are too fine to be woven together and stitching isn't strong enough”，这直接否定了编织和缝合这两种方法，因此B和C是错误的。

groups.desc: According to the speakers, what function has tapa cloth played in the following countries? Write the correct letter, A, B, C or D, next to questions 25 - 30.
groups.title: Questions 25-30

items.title: Samoa
items.analysis: 答案解析：第25题的正确答案是C（spiritual）。原文指出在“births, funerals, weddings and the investiture of chiefs”这些“profound events”中，萨摩亚人穿着西阿坡长袍以“add significance and meaning to the ceremony”，这 clearly indicates a spiritual or ceremonial function。

items.title: Tonga
items.analysis: 答案解析：第26题的正确答案是B（practical）。原文描述汤加的塔帕布“is suitable for all sorts of everyday functions around the house like bedcudders, mosquito nets and curtains”，这些都是日常家居用品， clearly indicates a practical function。

items.title: Cook Islands
items.analysis: 答案解析：第27题的正确答案是C（spiritual）。原文指出Tikoru这种布料“was wrapped around the poles and used to mark the entrances to places of worship so it was highly regarded in local culture”，这直接表明它在宗教场所使用并受到高度重视，具有精神或宗教意义。

items.title: Fiji
items.analysis: 答案解析：第28题的正确答案是D（commercial）。原文明确指出“Tupper was actually used as a currency there”并且“Fijians used to sail between the islands and exchange Tupper for other commodities like canoes or pigs”，这清楚地表明塔帕布在斐济具有商业交易和货币的功能。

items.title: Tahiti
items.analysis: 答案解析：第29题的正确答案是A（recreational）。在海伦提到彩色图案被认为更有价值之后，导师纠正说“they aren't more valuable. The colours are only a decoration. People enjoy wearing bright robes, especially for dancing and competitive games and do it just for fun.”，明确指出彩色图案仅用于装饰，人们穿它是为了跳舞、比赛和娱乐，这些都属于休闲娱乐功能，因此A是正确的。海伦认为“more valuable”是错误的观点，因此导师的纠正信息“they aren't more valuable”是区分干扰项的关键。

items.title: Tikopia
items.analysis: 答案解析：第30题的正确答案是B（practical）。原文指出“Even today it's common place to see people wearing clothes made of Tupper cloth”以及“you see people working in the gardens wearing Tupper”，这表明在Tikopia，塔帕布被用作日常穿着和工作服，是一种非常实用的功能。
